title: Display & Search Contacts
description: Add two functions to your contact manager: display_contacts() that prints all contacts in a formatted way, and find_contact(name) that searches by name (case-insensitive) and returns matching contacts.
starter_code:
# Contact Manager — Step 2: Display & Search
# Start with your contacts list from Step 1, then add functions.

contacts = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'phone': '555-0003', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'},
]

def display_contacts(contact_list):
    """Print all contacts in a formatted way."""
    # Your code here
    pass

def find_contact(contact_list, search_name):
    """Find contacts by name (case-insensitive partial match).
    Returns a list of matching contact dicts."""
    # Your code here
    return []

# Test it
display_contacts(contacts)
results = find_contact(contacts, 'alice')
print(f"Found {len(results)} match(es)")
test_code:
assert callable(display_contacts), 'display_contacts should be a function'
assert callable(find_contact), 'find_contact should be a function'
results = find_contact(contacts, 'alice')
assert len(results) == 1, f'find_contact for alice should return 1 result, got {len(results)}'
assert results[0]['name'] == 'Alice', f"Expected Alice, got {results[0]['name']}"
results2 = find_contact(contacts, 'ALICE')
assert len(results2) == 1, 'Search should be case-insensitive'
results3 = find_contact(contacts, 'nobody')
assert len(results3) == 0, 'Search for nonexistent should return empty list'
acceptance_criteria: display_contacts() prints contacts in a readable format
find_contact() returns matching contacts as a list
Returns empty list when no matches
hints: Use a for loop to iterate contacts in display_contacts
For case-insensitive search: if search_name.lower() in c['name'].lower()
Build a results list with a list comprehension or for loop
